title: Observable — Anatomy of a Black Hole
voice_suffix: clear enthusiastic science communicator voice, precise but accessible diction, synthetic presenter only

shots.video_prompt: CONTINUITY LOCK @Julian-001: identical presenter face, smart-casual wardrobe, seamless neutral set, same eye-line to camera — seamless continuation of prior take, zero jump cut. CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Seamless-Neutral-001: identical neutral grey seamless — same backdrop tone mid-grey #9A9A98, same floor sweep, same subject-to-backdrop distance, same falloff — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Cool-Clinical-001: cool-clinical grade — neutral white balance, low saturation, accurate color reproduction, minimal stylistic grading, science and product trust, no warm cozy cast, no film grain. Presenter addresses camera on seamless neutral, crisp science hook. Camera: locked medium, direct address. Neutral seamless 5500K bilateral soft key locked — symmetrical wrap, subtle nose shadow only, zero hue drift, no magenta vignette, no warm/cool lateral split. Neutral 5600–6500K even field — shadow definition for shape only, no gel, no warm practicals in frame. COLOR LOCK Seamless-Neutral: backdrop #9A9A98±2%, skin true-to-life, wardrobe colors accurate, no gel spill — no magenta edge gradient, no orange spotlight cone. GRADE LOCK Cool-Clinical: grey axis neutral, skin neither pink nor yellow pushed, saturation -15% global — forbid magenta tint, forbid orange warmth, forbid teal skin on shadows, forbid vintage fade. Locked-off or slow linear track; no handheld. Lip-synced, delivers: "What if you crossed a boundary where even light cannot climb back out? That is not science fiction — it is what a black hole does to spacetime." clear enthusiastic science communicator voice, precise but accessible diction, synthetic presenter only. Finish on presentational gesture peak, never hold dead stillness or dead frame.
shots.speech_text: What if you crossed a boundary where even light cannot climb back out? That is not science fiction — it is what a black hole does to spacetime.

CONTINUITY LOCK @Set-Seamless-Neutral-001: identical neutral grey seamless — same backdrop tone mid-grey #9A9A98, same floor sweep, same subject-to-backdrop distance, same falloff — seamless continuation, zero jump cut. STYLE LOCK @Style-Cool-Clinical-001: cool-clinical grade — neutral white balance, low saturation, accurate color reproduction, minimal stylistic grading, science and product trust, no warm cozy cast, no film grain. Presenter explains the phenomenon plainly, motivated gesture, clinical lighting. Camera: slow push-in medium. Neutral seamless 5500K bilateral soft key locked — symmetrical wrap, subtle nose shadow only, zero hue drift, no magenta vignette, no warm/cool lateral split. Neutral 5600–6500K even field — shadow definition for shape only, no gel, no warm practicals in frame. Locked-off or slow linear track; no handheld. Lip-synced, delivers: "A black hole is not a hole you fall through. Finish on presentational gesture peak, never hold dead stillness or dead frame.
shots.speech_text: A black hole is not a hole you fall through. It is mass so compressed that gravity curves space and time into a one-way boundary — the event horizon — with a dark shadow at the center and, often, a blazing accretion disk around it.

Neutral seamless 5500K bilateral soft key locked — symmetrical wrap, subtle nose shadow only, zero hue drift, no magenta vignette, no warm/cool lateral split. Neutral 5600–6500K even field — shadow definition for shape only, no gel, no warm practicals in frame. Lip-synced, delivers: "In twenty nineteen, the Event Horizon Telescope resolved the shadow of M87's supermassive black hole — forty-two micro-arcseconds wide. We observe the shadow and the waves — not the singularity inside." clear enthusiastic science communicator voice, precise but accessible diction, synthetic presenter only. shots.speech_text: In twenty nineteen, the Event Horizon Telescope resolved the shadow of M87's supermassive black hole — forty-two micro-arcseconds wide. LIGO has heard stellar-mass black holes merge, rippling spacetime itself. We observe the shadow and the waves — not the singularity inside.
